Title: Overexpression of a Malus baccata MYB Transcription Factor Gene MbMYB4 Increases Cold and Drought Tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MbMYB4 encodes a 253–amino acid hydrophilic protein with an isoelectric point of 6.84. The Malus baccata genome has not been sequenced, so cloning was based on homology to Malus domestica. MbMYB4 is classified as an R2R3-MYB transcription factor based on two SANT-MYB DNA-binding domains and similarity to other plant MYBs, and it localizes to the nucleus. Its expression is most strongly induced in young leaves and roots by cold and dehydration and is also responsive to ABA, suggesting a role at the interface of cold, drought, and ABA signaling.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MbMYB4 was cloned from Malus baccata as a 762 bp ORF encoding a 253–amino acid hydrophilic R2R3-MYB transcription factor with two SANT-MYB DNA-binding domains and nuclear localization.
2. MbMYB4 is expressed in multiple organs of M. baccata, with higher expression in roots and young leaves, and is strongly induced by low temperature, dehydration, and ABA, and to a lesser extent by high salt and high temperature.
3. Overexpression of MbMYB4 in Arabidopsis thaliana significantly improves cold tolerance, increasing survival after -6 °C treatment and enhancing chlorophyll and proline contents, POD and CAT activities, while reducing MDA accumulation and membrane damage.
4. Overexpression of MbMYB4 also improves drought tolerance in Arabidopsis, increasing survival after 10 d of water deprivation and improving physiological indices associated with osmotic adjustment and ROS scavenging.
5. Under cold stress, MbMYB4-overexpressing plants show elevated expression of AtCBF1, AtCBF3, AtCOR15a, and AtRD29a, indicating that MbMYB4 activates a CBF-dependent cold response pathway.
6. Under drought stress, MbMYB4-overexpressing plants show elevated expression of ABA-related genes AtNCED3 and AtSnRK2.4 and drought/ROS-related genes AtCAT1 and AtP5CS, suggesting that MbMYB4 enhances drought tolerance through ABA biosynthesis/signaling and improved ROS detoxification and proline accumulation.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MbMYB4 is a nuclear R2R3-MYB transcription factor whose overexpression confers enhanced cold and drought tolerance, as evidenced by higher survival rates, better maintenance of chlorophyll, increased proline, POD and CAT activities, and reduced MDA content and membrane damage under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a nuclear transcriptional regulator that, when overexpressed, positively regulates expression of cold-responsive genes AtCBF1 and AtCBF3 and their targets AtCOR15a and AtRD29a under cold stress, and upregulates ABA biosynthesis and signaling genes AtNCED3 and AtSnRK2.4 as well as drought/ROS-related genes AtCAT1 and AtP5CS under drought, thereby enhancing ROS scavenging and osmotic adjustment.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Participates in a CBF-dependent cold response pathway by acting upstream of AtCBF1/AtCBF3 and their downstream cold-responsive genes AtCOR15a and AtRD29a, and in ABA-mediated drought response by promoting ABA biosynthesis and signaling via AtNCED3 and AtSnRK2.4 and enhancing ROS detoxification and proline accumulation via AtCAT1 and AtP5CS.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Positively influences ABA biosynthesis via upregulation of AtNCED3 and promotes proline biosynthesis via activation of AtP5CS under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: MbMYB4 -->(activation)--> AtCBF1; MbMYB4 -->(activation)--> AtCBF3; AtCBF1 -->(activation)--> AtCOR15a; AtCBF1 -->(activation)--> AtRD29a; AtCBF3 -->(activation)--> AtCOR15a; AtCBF3 -->(activation)--> AtRD29a; MbMYB4 -->(activation)--> AtNCED3; MbMYB4 -->(activation)--> AtSnRK2.4; MbMYB4 -->(activation)--> AtCAT1; MbMYB4 -->(activation)--> AtP5CS.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor containing two SANT-MYB DNA-binding domains with helix-turn-helix structure.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, demonstrated by MbMYB4-GFP fusion protein localization in onion epidermal cells co-localizing with DAPI-stained nuclei.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: MbMYB4 is expressed in young leaf, stem, root, and mature leaf of Malus baccata, with higher basal expression in root and young leaf than in mature leaf and stem. Its transcript level in young leaf and root is strongly induced by low temperature, high-salt, dehydration, high temperature, and ABA treatments, with the highest induction under cold and dehydration stresses (up to about 7.8–8.7-fold over control at peak times).
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-temperature stress at 4 °C, high-salt stress with 200 mM NaCl, dehydration stress with 20% PEG6000, high-temperature stress at 37 °C, and ABA treatment at 50 μM for Malus baccata seedlings; cold stress of -6 °C for 14 h and drought stress by withholding water for 10 days for Arabidopsis thaliana overexpressing MbMYB4.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold and drought) and transcriptional regulation in Malus baccata using the MYB transcription factor MbMYB4.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold tolerance and drought tolerance); ABA response.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In Malus baccata, MbMYB4 expression in young leaf and root under low temperature and dehydration reaches about 7.5–8.7-fold higher than untreated controls at peak times. Under cold stress at -6 °C, survival of MbMYB4-overexpressing Arabidopsis averages 77% versus 36% for WT and 26% for UL. Under drought stress (10 d without water), survival of transgenic lines S1, S3, and S5 is 76%, 74%, and 85%, respectively, versus 27% for WT and 49% for UL.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under cold and drought stresses, MbMYB4-overexpressing Arabidopsis plants show higher activities of POD and CAT, higher proline and chlorophyll contents, and lower MDA content and relative conductivity compared with WT and UL plants, indicating reduced oxidative damage and membrane injury and better maintenance of photosynthetic capacity.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: In vitro–propagated Malus baccata seedlings for expression analysis and stress treatments; T3 homozygous Arabidopsis thaliana Columbia wild-type background lines overexpressing MbMYB4 (S1, S3, S5) plus wild-type (WT) and empty vector line (UL) as controls; onion epidermal cells for transient expression of 35S:MbMYB4-GFP for subcellular localization.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Homology-based cloning of MbMYB4 from Malus baccata cDNA using MdMYB25 CDS as reference; sequence and domain analysis; subcellular localization by 35S:MbMYB4-GFP transient expression in onion epidermal cells with DAPI nuclear staining; qPCR to profile MbMYB4 expression in different organs and under low temperature (4 °C), high salt (200 mM NaCl), dehydration (20% PEG6000), high temperature (37 °C), and ABA (50 μM) treatments; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of Arabidopsis thaliana with pCAMBIA2300-MbMYB4 and selection to T3; cold stress (-6 °C, 14 h) and drought stress (10 d without watering) assays; measurement of chlorophyll, proline, POD, CAT, MDA, and relative conductivity; qPCR of downstream cold-related genes (AtCBF1, AtCBF3, AtCOR15a, AtRD29a) and drought/ABA-related genes (AtNCED3, AtSnRK2.4, AtCAT1, AtP5CS).
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by constitutive overexpression of MbMYB4 in Arabidopsis thaliana (pCAMBIA2300-MbMYB4, T3 homozygous lines S1, S3, S5) followed by cold stress (-6 °C for 14 h) and drought stress (10 d without watering) assays, physiological index measurements (proline, chlorophyll, POD, CAT, MDA, relative conductivity), and qPCR analysis of stress-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Homology-based cloning using the CDS of Malus domestica MdMYB25 as reference, RT-PCR amplification of MbMYB4 from Malus baccata cDNA with specific primers, cloning into pEASY-T1 vector, and sequencing.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Overexpression of MbMYB4 significantly enhances cold and drought tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis thaliana, indicating that MbMYB4 is a promising candidate gene for engineering or breeding Malus and other crops with improved tolerance to low temperature and water deficit conditions, particularly in cold and arid regions.
